title: Add CodeWiki architecture review workflow adapted from deep-module heuristics
summary: Adapt deep-module and shared-language architecture review ideas into a CodeWiki-aware workflow for finding seams, locality problems, and roadmap-worthy refactors.

goal.outcome: CodeWiki can run architecture reviews that produce knowledge updates and roadmap tasks rather than ad hoc refactor suggestions.
acceptance: Workflow uses CodeWiki vocabulary and `.codewiki/kb` instead of `CONTEXT.md`/ADR assumptions.
Review rubric includes module/interface/implementation, seam, adapter, locality, leverage, and deletion test.
Findings become either knowledge clarifications, roadmap tasks, or explicitly deferred non-goals.
Architecture review does not propose interfaces before presenting friction candidates and asking user to pick.
non_goals: Create a separate ADR tree outside `.codewiki/kb`.
verification: Run the workflow on the CodeWiki repo and produce at least one candidate or documented no-op result.
Confirm any resulting tasks link to owning specs and acceptance criteria.

delta.desired: Architecture improvement becomes a first-class CodeWiki planning input.
delta.current: General architecture heuristics are useful but not adapted to CodeWiki's knowledge/roadmap model.
delta.closure: Add CodeWiki-specific architecture review skill or section and validate against the repo.
